title: arctotis frutescens
intro: Arctotis frutescens, also known as the leopard's ear or king's cigar, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Arctotis frutescens include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and supporting immune function. Arctotis frutescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
